quick_skim.title: Efficient Domain-Adaptive Policy Learning via Kernel Representation with Application to Quadrotor Control under Non-Stationary Disturbances
quick_skim.abstract: We present an algorithm for efficient domain-adaptive policy learning via kernel representations. Learning domain-adaptive policies is challenging since it requires an environment representation that is both sufficiently expressive to model complex sim-to-real gaps during offline training, and computationally efficient enough to support rapid online adaptation during deployment. For instance, a quadrotor may encounter time-varying, non-stationary disturbances, such as sudden gusts of wind, payload shifts, or transitions between distinct flight regimes with and without ground effects. To address these challenges, we model unknown disturbances using a differentiable kernel approximation based on random Fourier features. During the offline training phase, we randomly sample kernel coefficients and bandwidth parameters to generate a rich diversity of disturbance profiles. We then optimize the control policy via differentiable simulation with analytical gradients, a process that takes only 50 seconds of training time on an RTX 4090 GPU. During hardware deployment, the policy adapts to non-stationary environments in real time by updating both the kernel coefficients and bandwidth through online least-squares estimation. We evaluate our method on quadrotor trajectory tracking tasks across high-fidelity numerical simulations and hardware experiments using Crazyflie, subjected to various disturbances, including complex aerodynamic effects, wind, ground effects, and payload fluctuations.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: domain-adaptive policy learning for quadrotor control under disturbances relevant to pursuit-evasion
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向追逃场景的四旋翼控制域自适应策略学习
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向追逃场景的四旋翼控制域自适应策略学习

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper presents a kernel-based domain-adaptive RL method for quadrotor control under non-stationary disturbances, useful for pursuit-evasion where wind or payload changes occur.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出了一种基于核表示的域自适应策略学习算法，用于应对四旋翼飞行器在非平稳扰动（如阵风、载荷变化）下的控制问题。该方法通过可微核近似建模未知扰动，支持快速的在线适应。该工作与无人机追逃博弈中的动态环境适应高度相关。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出了一种基于核表示的域自适应策略学习算法，用于应对四旋翼飞行器在非平稳扰动（如阵风、载荷变化）下的控制问题。该方法通过可微核近似建模未知扰动，支持快速的在线适应。该工作与无人机追逃博弈中的动态环境适应高度相关。

quick_skim.title: Wind-Resilient Trajectory Optimization for UAV-BS Networks: TD3 for Continuous Service Availability
quick_skim.abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV)-mounted base stations are highly susceptible to wind disturbances such as gusts and turbulence, which induce positional drift and degrade communication link quality, particularly in emergency scenarios. To address this challenge, we propose a DRL-based framework for wind-resilient trajectory adjustment and positioning based on the Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TD3) algorithm. The method models wind as a stochastic kinematic perturbation, avoiding complex aerodynamic modeling, thereby enabling the TD3 agent to learn adaptive control policies that maintain optimal coverage footprints. By prioritizing user-centric performance metrics under turbulent conditions, the proposed architecture ensures continuous service availability despite external disruptions. Simulation results demonstrate that the TD3-based approach effectively compensates for wind-induced displacements and outperforms benchmark methods, including Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO), in terms of throughput stability and robustness in windy environments.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: DRL-based trajectory optimization for UAV under wind disturbances
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于深度强化学习的无人机轨迹优化以应对风扰
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于深度强化学习的无人机轨迹优化以应对风扰
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于深度强化学习的无人机轨迹优化以应对风扰
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper uses TD3 to optimize UAV trajectory for communication coverage under wind disturbances, demonstrating DRL for adaptive UAV control relevant to interception scenarios.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出了一种基于TD3算法的无人机轨迹优化框架，用于在风扰下保持通信覆盖。它将风建模为随机运动学扰动，使智能体学习自适应控制策略。该方法虽针对通信任务，但其DRL控制机制可迁移至无人机拦截和围捕中的轨迹规划。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出了一种基于TD3算法的无人机轨迹优化框架，用于在风扰下保持通信覆盖。它将风建模为随机运动学扰动，使智能体学习自适应控制策略。该方法虽针对通信任务，但其DRL控制机制可迁移至无人机拦截和围捕中的轨迹规划。

quick_skim.title: Formal Verification of Learned Multi-Agent Communication Policies via Decision Tree Distillation
quick_skim.abstract: Multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) enables agents to develop coordination strategies through emergent communication, but neural policies lack the formal safety guarantees required for safety-critical robotic deployment in drone swarms and autonomous vehicle fleets. We present the first end-to-end framework for safety verification of learned multi-agent communication policies through policy abstraction: neural policies are distilled into interpretable decision trees, then formally verified, with empirical validation confirming that verified safety properties transfer to original networks. Our four-stage pipeline consists of domain-specific feature extraction from agent observations, decision tree distillation achieving 97.9% +/- 1.2% fidelity to neural policies, automated translation to PRISM probabilistic model checker specifications with complete feature-to-state-variable correspondence, and compositional verification of Probabilistic Computation Tree Logic (PCTL) properties via pairwise decomposition with union-bound aggregation and empirical neighbor modeling. Evaluating Vector-Quantized Variational Information Bottleneck (VQ-VIB) policies for multi-drone coordination with 5-7 agents, we verify 18 temporal logic properties across safety, liveness, and cooperation, achieving 88.9% property satisfaction with all five safety thresholds satisfied (0.3% collision probability vs. 1% threshold). Monte Carlo validation of original neural policies confirms that verified safety properties transfer with <=0.6 percentage-point deviation (95% CI). Discrete VQ-VIB messages provide +11.6 to +13.6 percentage-point fidelity advantages over continuous methods, enabling 3-4x faster verification. Our framework provides empirically validated safety verification for distilled policy abstractions, serving as a practical bridge between deep MARL and formal safety workflows for multi-robot deployment.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: MARL with decision tree verification for drone swarm safety
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 针对无人机群的多智能体强化学习及决策树验证
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 针对无人机群的多智能体强化学习及决策树验证
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 针对无人机群的多智能体强化学习及决策树验证
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes first end-to-end framework for safety verification of learned multi-agent communication policies, applied to drone swarms, using decision tree distillation and formal verification.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出了首个针对多智能体通信策略的安全验证框架，通过决策树蒸馏将神经网络策略转化为可解释模型并形式化验证。实验证明验证的安全属性可迁移回原始网络，为无人机群等安全关键应用提供了保障。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出了首个针对多智能体通信策略的安全验证框架，通过决策树蒸馏将神经网络策略转化为可解释模型并形式化验证。实验证明验证的安全属性可迁移回原始网络，为无人机群等安全关键应用提供了保障。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: multi-agent reinforcement learning for UAV swarm

quick_skim.title: Hierarchical Control in Multi-Agent Games: LLM-based Planning and RL Execution
quick_skim.abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) has achieved strong performance in sequential decision-making, yet scaling to complex multi-agent environments remains challenging due to sparse rewards, large state-action spaces, and the difficulty of learning coordinated strategies. We propose a hierarchical architecture where a pretrained large language model (LLM) acts as a centralized strategic controller that selects among specialized RL skill policies for a team of agents, while RL policies handle reactive low-level execution. We evaluate this hybrid system in a competitive 2v2 King of the Hill environment against behavior tree (BT) and \emph{``Flat''} RL (end-to-end training without skill decomposition) baselines. The LLM+RL system achieves task performance statistically equivalent to hand-crafted BT (46.4\% vs 51.5\% win rate, $p=0.103$) while both significantly outperform Flat RL trained without skill decomposition. A user study ($n=15$) reveals that 60\% of participants perceive LLM+RL agents as the most human-like ($p=0.027$), citing behavioral adaptability and tactical variability. These results demonstrate that pretrained LLM reasoning can effectively orchestrate pretrained RL skills, achieving competitive multi-agent coordination and superior perceived believability without manual rule engineering.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Multi-agent RL with hierarchical LLM+RL, applicable to UAV swarm coordination
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多智能体分层LLM+RL架构，可应用于无人机群协调
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多智能体分层LLM+RL架构，可应用于无人机群协调
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多智能体分层LLM+RL架构，可应用于无人机群协调
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes hierarchical architecture with LLM planner and RL skill policies for multi-agent games, improving coordination.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对多智能体游戏中稀疏奖励和大状态空间导致的协调困难，提出一种分层架构：预训练大语言模型作为中央策略控制器选择RL技能策略，RL策略负责底层执行。在2v2 King of the Hill环境中，该方法优于行为树和端到端RL基线，实现了更好的任务性能。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对多智能体游戏中稀疏奖励和大状态空间导致的协调困难，提出一种分层架构：预训练大语言模型作为中央策略控制器选择RL技能策略，RL策略负责底层执行。在2v2 King of the Hill环境中，该方法优于行为树和端到端RL基线，实现了更好的任务性能。
